title: Floor plan of the House of the Vettii, Pompeii (VI 15,1)
creator: August Mau
rightsholder_or_source: Public-domain plate from Mau, 'Pompeii: Its Life and Art' (1907); via Wikimedia Commons
research_notes: Lettered room key (legend) instead of drawn dimensions; courtyard-with-hole geometry.
research_features: Classic Roman domus: entrance fauces, atrium with central impluvium, flanking cubicula and alae, tablinum, and a large colonnaded peristyle GARDEN COURTYARD (a room/polygon with a central hole). Rooms labeled with letters keyed to a legend rather than dimensions.
feature_highlights: classic Roman domus sequence: fauces, atrium, tablinum, peristyle
atrium with a central impluvium basin (small open-air court)
large colonnaded peristyle garden courtyard as a room polygon with a hole
regular ring of columns drawn as dots around the garden ambulatory
every room labeled with a single letter keyed to a printed legend
compass rose north arrow and metric graphic scale bar
section line A-B drawn across the plan
thick masonry walls in black poché, all straight segments
supported_examples: straight walls forming many small rectangular rooms (cubicula, alae)
room polygons and custom-polygon overrides for irregular rooms
raster underlay with scale calibration to trace the historic plate
roadmap_examples: wall construction profiles / poché for thick masonry walls
site/textual metadata for a named excavated house

gap_features.why: the peristyle is a covered ring around an open garden (a room polygon with a hole) and the atrium is a smaller open-air court with an impluvium
the colonnaded ambulatory around the peristyle garden is a roofed-but-open walkway, not a fully wall-enclosed room
rooms are identified entirely by single letters keyed to a separate legend, with no inscribed dimensions on the plan
the plate carries a compass-rose north arrow and a graphic metric scale bar as drawn annotations
